core_payload.content:
# A2C Candidate Construction

## 1. Role
A2C Candidate Construction owns the graph-aware reasoning layer that assembles capsule candidates and situates them against live memory. It turns extracted evidence into a candidate payload, then tests that candidate against the existing graph before any sealing or admission step occurs.

## 2. Candidate Construction Surface
This sub-hub groups `a2c-synthesize`, `a2c-link`, and `n-infinity`. Together they define how A2C assembles candidate structure through prompt law, compares that structure against live graph reality, and draws on swarm reasoning support where topology, contradiction pressure, or graph context becomes materially important.

## 3. Operating Boundary
This sub-hub does not control pre-mutation readiness, policy interpretation, or final sealing and emission. Those responsibilities belong to `readiness-control`, `governance-handoffs`, and `admission-emission`.

neuro_concentrate.summary: A2C Candidate Construction groups the graph-aware reasoning surfaces of the refinery: Synthesize, Link, and N-Infinity support. It keeps candidate assembly, graph placement, and contradiction-sensitive structure reasoning together so A2C can build a capsule proposal that is aware of live memory before validation or publication begins. This boundary matters because candidate construction should stay distinct from raw-material handling on one side and policy-bearing admission decisions on the other. It preserves a legible candidate-making seam inside the refinery.

epistemic_ledger.rationale: Minted during A2C decomposition to isolate candidate assembly and graph placement from raw material preparation and downstream governance or emission gates.
